title: Assertion `memIsValid(&aMem[pOp->p1])' failed
description:
yongheng added on 2020-02-29 21:39:43:
Consider the following POC:
---
CREATE TABLE v0(v2, v1);
CREATE TABLE v3(v4, v5);
CREATE INDEX v6 ON v3(v5, v4);
INSERT INTO v0 VALUES(0, 8);
SELECT(SELECT MIN(v4) FROM v0 LEFT JOIN v3 ON NULL = v5)
---

It triggers an assertion in debug build of trunk.
drh added on 2020-03-02 01:30:39:
A bytecode register was used without first being initialized in some
obscure circumstances when using ORDER BY ... NULLS LAST on a LEFT JOIN.
Luckily, the
NULL value in the uninitialized register was interpreted as a zero, and so
the bytecode still computed the correct answer.  However, when compiling
with assert()s enabled, there is an assert() that detects the initialized
register reference.
The fix was to initialize the register to zero for LEFT JOINs.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1(a INT, b VARCHAR(255), c FLOAT);
CREATE TABLE t2(x INT, y INT, z VARCHAR(255));
SELECT (SELECT MIN(x) FROM t1 LEFT JOIN t2 ON t1.a = t2.x ORDER BY t2.y IS NULL, t2.y);
key_bug_features: Uninitialized bytecode register in LEFT JOIN with ORDER BY
